channel.title: Cointelegraph.com News
channel.description: Cointelegraph covers fintech, blockchain and Bitcoin bringing you the latest crypto news and analyses on the future of money.

image.title: Cointelegraph.com News
image.description: Cointelegraph covers fintech, blockchain and Bitcoin bringing you the latest crypto news and analyses on the future of money.

item.title: Crypto card monthly transaction volume surges 230% from 2025
item.description: Payment volume on crypto-linked credit and debit cards has been steadily increasing since 2024, reaching about $7.8 billion in cumulative transactions this month.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Vince Quill

item.title: Bitcoin miner inflows to Binance soar as BTC struggles to hold uptrend: Is $70K next?
item.description: Weakening spot demand, miner inflows to exchanges and freshly opened shorts put downside pressure on Bitcoin price.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Biraajmaan Tamuly

item.title: Polymarket weighs KYC requirements amid global crackdown on prediction markets
item.description: The predictions market operator reportedly explored mandatory user verification requirements, breaking from its policies of allowing traders to access its services using pseudonyms.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Turner Wright

item.title: Orca, Streamex roll out secondary trading infrastructure for tokenized securities
item.description: Accredited investors can buy and sell the gold-backed GLDY token through permissioned liquidity pools operating on the Solana blockchain.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Nate Kostar

item.title: PACs laud Texas primary wins, look to back more pro-crypto candidates
item.description: While crypto-backed candidates won runoffs in Texas on Tuesday, industry PACs have less of a stake in California races next week.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Turner Wright

item.title: Bitcoin treasury company Nakamoto falls nearly 67% YTD after reverse stock split
item.description: The company owns 5,058 Bitcoin, ranking it as the 20th largest publicly traded BTC treasury company, according to data from Bitcoin Treasuries.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Vince Quill

item.title: Ether bears at risk of $2B squeeze as short positions build around $2K
item.description: Ether futures positioning tightened near $2,000 as rising open interest and dense short liquidity increased focus on a possible squeeze above $2,150.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Biraajmaan Tamuly

item.title: Price predictions 5/27: BTC, ETH, BNB, XRP, SOL, DOGE, HYPE, ZEC, ADA, XMR
item.description: Bitcoin briefly lost the $75,000 level after net flows into spot BTC ETFs turned negative. Do technical charts point to a BTC and altcoin recovery?
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Rakesh Upadhyay

item.title: Mastercard secures New York BitLicense for crypto operations
item.description: The payments giant can now legally conduct digital asset business activity in New York as it deepens its focus on blockchain-based settlement systems.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Sam Bourgi

item.title: Prediction markets enter the derivatives era: XBIT DEX opens whitelist for prediction leverage, launching a 35,000 USDC campaign
item.description: XBIT DEX takes the lead in introducing leverage to on-chain prediction markets, prioritizing the 2026 FIFA World Cup for its initial category. The whitelist application is now live.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Advertorial

item.title: Bitcoin price threatens $75K loss as US-Iran peace progress sparks new stocks records
item.description: Bitcoin disappointed bulls by dropping below $75,000 on Iran peace deal reports while US stocks hit new all-time highs and oil saw one-month lows on Hormuz hopes.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by William Suberg

item.title: BTC Prague 2026: Europe’s leading Bitcoin conference expands its cultural reach
item.description: BTC Prague, Europe’s premier Bitcoin conference, returns to the PVA Expo Prague on June 11-13, 2026.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Advertorial

item.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
item.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Cointelegraph

item.title: StakeDAO exploit creates 5.4 trillion vsdCRV but nets only $91K
item.description: PeckShield said the attacker bridged 43.7 ETH to Ethereum after minting trillions of vsdCRV, while EmberCN said most of the remaining tokens had insufficient liquidity to sell.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Ezra Reguerra

item.title: HTX denies UK sanctions allegations as new data flags $7.6B Russia-linked flows
item.description: UK sanctions Huobi Global S.A., operator of HTX, over alleged role in Russia’s “A7” shadow network, as new analysis claims the exchange handled billions in high‑risk flows.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Christina Comben

item.title: Banca Sella gets MiCA clearance for crypto services in Italy
item.description: The bank plans to launch digital asset custody, transfer and receipt services in 2026 for selected customer categories.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Ezra Reguerra

item.title: Bitcoin price shrugs off $1.3B BlackRock ETF block sale
item.description: Bitcoin’s price stayed firm despite a massive $1.3 billion block sale executed by a mysterious BlackRock ETF holder, which analysts called a sign of large-scale institutional de-risking.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Zoltan Vardai

item.title: Sold in May and went away? Bitcoin risks another 10% drop as month turns red
item.description: Bitcoin is on track to end May in the red, a historically bearish signal that could point to deeper losses ahead if the post-May track record is any guide.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Yashu Gola

item.title: Aeternum brings the second edition of Unchained Summit to Vietnam on May 28-29
item.description: Aeternum, organisers of the Unchained Summit series, will host the second edition of Unchained Summit Vietnam this week on May 28 and 29 at the Furama Resort in Da Nang.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Advertorial

item.title: China’s top court to study rules for crypto and AI cases
item.description: China’s Supreme People’s Court plans to study adjudication rules for crypto and AI cases while the country’s crypto ban remains in force.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Zoltan Vardai

item.title: Crypto-backed candidates win key Texas primary runoffs
item.description: Crypto PACs threw serious money behind a handful of Texas runoffs, helping oust a senior Democrat and boost Republican hopefuls as lawmakers hammer out new US crypto rules.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Christina Comben

item.title: Three key XRP metrics suggest ‘explosive price expansion’ is next
item.description: Falling MVRV ratio, high XRP Ledger activity and a bullish wedge pattern signal a potential XRP price rise toward $3.10.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Nancy Lubale

item.title: ETHConf brings Ethereum’s leaders, institutions, and policymakers to New York City on June 8–10
item.description: ETHConf, produced by ETHGlobal, will bring more than 5,000 attendees, 150+ speakers, and 100+ companies to the Javits Center in New York City from June 8-10, 2026 for three days focused on the future of Ethereum and institutional finance.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Advertorial

item.title: Spot HYPE ETFs absorb 1% of market cap in first 10 trading days: Kairos
item.description: Spot HYPE ETFs absorbed 1.04% of Hyperliquid’s market cap in 10 trading days, beating Bitcoin and Ether ETF debuts.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Ezra Reguerra

item.title: Bitcoin analysis eyes sharp rebound after BTC collapses below M2 supply 'fair value'
item.description: Bitcoin remained trapped "massively below" its "fair" level, as dictated by global liquidity trends and gold ratio, the latest BTC price analysis says.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by William Suberg

item.title: South Korea charges CATFI memecoin operators in first DEX rug-pull case: Report
item.description: South Korean prosecutors reportedly charged a group over the CATFI memecoin rug pull, marking the country’s first DEX rug-pull case under crypto law.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Zoltan Vardai

item.title: Ethereum bull David Hoffman explains why he sold his ETH
item.description: “Ethereum got the ETH price it deserves, and I don’t see ETH being rerated as an asset, higher or lower,” says David Hoffman.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Martin Young

item.title: Falcon Finance, SoFi unveil stablecoin products tied to banking infrastructure
item.description: While Falcon’s new fUSD is built for institutional trading and collateral workflows, SoFiUSD targets consumer banking users through SoFi’s app.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Nate Kostar

item.title: Sharplink, Forward Industries among crypto firms considered for Russell indexes
item.description: Bitmine and Galaxy Digital may also be eligible for the Russell 1000, an index tracking the largest 1,000 US companies that includes Nvidia, Microsoft and Apple.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Stephen Katte

item.title: Bitcoin’s recent drop coincides with $1.3B ‘dark pool’ ETF sale: Analyst
item.description: Galaxy Digital’s Alex Thorn says a $1.3 billion sale of BlackRock’s Bitcoin ETF was the largest he has seen on a dark pool, or private trading platform.
item.dc:creator: Cointelegraph by Brayden Lindrea
